hypothesis: Augmenting CHROMA with a differentiable continuous-thickness refinement module that, given the decoder’s discrete material sequence, performs 50 gradient-descent steps on continuous thicknesses using the differentiable TMM and a sequence-level CIE76 color-loss will reduce mean validation ∆E on held-out structures by more than 50% relative to the baseline CHROMA (frozen encoder, 8-layer decoder, discrete-only outputs).
